standards.description: Develop and use a model to describe the function of a cell as a whole and ways parts of cells contribute to the function.

visual_surface.image_prompt: Middle school life science animal cell illustration with visible organelles, no text baked into the image, open space for overlay labels.

annotations.label: Nucleus
annotations.description: Stores DNA and controls many cell activities.

annotations.label: Mitochondria
annotations.description: Release usable energy from food molecules.

annotations.label: Cell membrane
annotations.description: Controls what enters and leaves the cell.

annotations.label: Golgi apparatus
annotations.description: Packages and ships materials around or out of the cell.

content.objective: Identify major organelles and connect each structure to its function.
content.teacher_note: Students should use the overlay labels first, then switch to practice mode and explain functions from memory.

study_mode.prompt: Select each label and study how the organelle structure supports the cell's needs.

practice_mode.prompt: Hide labels, choose an organelle, and explain its role in the cell system.

checks.question: Which organelle is most directly responsible for releasing usable energy from food?

feedback.correct: Yes. Mitochondria release usable energy from food molecules.
feedback.incorrect: Look for the organelle connected to energy release.

reflection_prompt.prompt: Choose two organelles and explain how they work together to help the cell function.

standards.description: Develop a model to describe the cycling of water through Earth's systems driven by energy from the sun and force of gravity.

visual_surface.image_prompt: Middle school Earth science water cycle landscape with mountains, ocean, clouds, precipitation, runoff, infiltration, and groundwater; no labels baked into the image.

annotations.label: Evaporation
annotations.description: Sunlight adds energy and liquid water changes into water vapor.

annotations.label: Condensation
annotations.description: Water vapor cools and forms cloud droplets.

annotations.label: Precipitation
annotations.description: Water falls from clouds as rain, snow, sleet, or hail.

annotations.label: Runoff
annotations.description: Gravity moves water over land into rivers, lakes, and oceans.

annotations.label: Infiltration
annotations.description: Water soaks into soil and can become groundwater.

content.objective: Use a systems model to explain how energy and gravity move water through Earth systems.

study_mode.prompt: Trace each water movement and name the energy source or force that drives it.

practice_mode.prompt: Hide the labels and explain how water could travel from ocean to groundwater.

checks.question: Which process is driven mainly by energy from the sun?

feedback.correct: Right. Solar energy helps liquid water become water vapor.
feedback.incorrect: Think about the process where water gains energy and changes state.

reflection_prompt.prompt: Explain how the sun and gravity each affect one part of the water cycle.
